FILM GRAIN (Stochastic) - OFX plugin  -  v1.0  (macOS, test build)
====================================================================

A physically-based film-grain plugin with optional halation, tuned to
Kodak Vision3 500T. Works in DaVinci Resolve and other OpenFX hosts.

Requirements
------------
- macOS (Apple Silicon or Intel - the plugin is universal)
- DaVinci Resolve (free or Studio) or another OpenFX host
- ~1 MB disk


INSTALL (easy way)
------------------
1. Double-click  "Install Film Grain.command".

   First time, macOS may say it's from an unidentified developer and refuse.
   If so:  RIGHT-CLICK the file  >  Open  >  Open.  (You only do this once.)
   This build is ad-hoc signed but NOT notarized - that warning is expected
   for a test build and is safe to bypass.

2. Enter your Mac password when asked (needed to write to the system
   plug-ins folder).

3. QUIT and REOPEN DaVinci Resolve completely. It only scans for OFX
   plug-ins when it launches - closing the project is not enough.

4. In the color page, open the OpenFX library and look under the group
   "Fenner"  >  "Grain Match".  Drag it onto a clip or node.


INSTALL (manual, if you prefer Terminal)
----------------------------------------
   sudo cp -R FilmGrain.ofx.bundle /Library/OFX/Plugins/
   sudo xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Library/OFX/Plugins/FilmGrain.ofx.bundle
Then restart Resolve.


INSTALL (Windows)
-----------------
Requires an NVIDIA GPU for the accelerated path (other GPUs run on CPU).
1. RIGHT-CLICK "Install (Windows).bat"  >  "Run as administrator".
   (Or manually copy the FilmGrain.ofx.bundle folder into
    C:\Program Files\Common Files\OFX\Plugins\ )
2. Quit and reopen DaVinci Resolve.
3. Find it under  OpenFX > Fenner > Grain Match.


QUICK START
-----------
- Defaults are calibrated to Super 16 500T (7219) - drop it on and it should
  look right immediately.
- FILM FORMAT: switch to "Super 35 (5219)" for finer, cleaner 35mm grain.
- AMOUNT: overall grain strength (1.0 ~ matches the reference scan).
- GRAIN SIZE / STRUCTURE: size and large-scale clumping ("depth").
- COLOR COUPLING: how shared the grain is across R/G/B (high = filmic,
  less chroma noise).
- HALATION (collapsed group, OFF by default): the warm red glow around
  bright lights (CineStill-style). Raise "Halation Intensity" to ~0.3-0.5
  on a shot with practical lights. If you put the node early in the chain
  on log/flat footage and see no glow, raise "Highlight Gain" to 2-4.

PERFORMANCE
-----------
GPU-accelerated (Metal). If playback is heavy on your machine: lower Quality,
reduce Grain Structure / Color Coupling, or enable "Monochrome Grain". 4K is
heavier than 1080p - use Resolve's render cache if needed.

UNINSTALL
---------
Double-click "Uninstall Film Grain.command"  (or delete
/Library/OFX/Plugins/FilmGrain.ofx.bundle), then restart Resolve.

FEEDBACK
--------
This is a test build - please report what stock/footage you tried, how it
looked, and any crashes or load failures.

(c) 2026 Chris Fenner.  See LICENSE.txt.
